ops.title: Focus deck: untallied steps are ticks; one tally rail per screen
ops.body: Design decision (7b3d289, 2026-07-18), resolving the two focus-deck papercuts: steps with `stepStatus() === null` (note-only context runs, input-only steps) render as 4px muted ticks in the FocusProgress rail — padded back out to the decision dot's 26/30px hit area — and SubmitBar's per-item tally strip renders only on the board view (`showTally` prop, app passes `mode !== 'focus'`), so each screen has one progress vocabulary: "Step N / M" is card position, dots/tally are decision units. Accepted tradeoff: the strip's segment-tap jump straight to a nested decidable no longer exists in focus mode; next-undecided stepping and the in-card cursor ring still reach nested items — do not "fix" this by re-adding a per-item rail to focus mode. iOS parity is task 95075c09.
